# WORKBENCH Doc Standard

**Surface:** WORKBENCH is the canonical **build-surface** of every
venture/system that has one (per [JOS-C47]). Content here is
build-time: messy, transient, iterative. Files in WORKBENCH may
eventually be PROMOTED to operating-surface artifacts (via
[JOS-METHOD-WORKBENCH-LIFECYCLE]), but they aren't subject to
operating-surface governance while they live here. This standard
prescribes WORKBENCH discipline — light governance, just enough to
keep the build-surface coherent and queryable.

## Folder structure

```
WORKBENCH/
  README.md                    # folder rules + frontmatter spec + roster (per template)
  <person>/                    # tim/, van/, etc. — added as humans come in
    random/                    # the ONE catch-all per person — no orphan folders
      archive/                 # per-folder archive
    proj-<pm-shortname>/       # name MUST match a PM project shortname
      archive/                 # per-folder archive
```

### Folder discipline rules

1. **Every subfolder under `<person>/` is either `random/` OR matches
   a PM project shortname.** No orphan folders. PM project shortname
   lookups go via PM API (see [JOS-C-Item-20] `workbench_path` field
   on PM projects table).
2. **If a `random/` file matures into being project-bound**, it must
   move into the corresponding `proj-<shortname>/` folder AND get the
   `project:` frontmatter field filled in.
3. **Per-folder `archive/`** — not one mega-archive per person.
   Re-engaging a project after months means its archive is right there
   with the active stuff. Status frontmatter must agree with folder
   location: files in `archive/` have `status: archived`; files
   outside `archive/` have `status: active`.
4. **PM linkage** — folder names match PM project shortnames. PM
   projects table carries optional `workbench_path` for reverse lookup
   (PM project → its WORKBENCH folder).

## Required frontmatter (every `.md` in WORKBENCH/)

```yaml
---
doc_kind: research | planning | scratch | discovery | handoff | braindump | reference | note | gold
status: active | archived
project: <pm-shortname>        # null ONLY if file is in random/
owner: tim | van | shared
date_created: 2026-05-XX       # immutable; set at file birth
date_modified: 2026-05-XX      # auto-stamped by pre-commit hook
---
```

### `doc_kind` enum (LOCKED — 9 values)

- **`research`** — gathering info from external sources, exploring a
  domain
- **`planning`** — drafting an approach for a piece of work
- **`scratch`** — temp notes during active work
- **`discovery`** — exploration / spike (timeboxed)
- **`handoff`** — passing context from one person/agent to another
- **`braindump`** — unstructured dump (intuitive naming, renamed from
  "dump")
- **`reference`** — saved external resource for later reference
- **`note`** — general note that doesn't fit elsewhere
- **`gold`** — high-signal distillation worth re-reading (the "gold
  file" — files you find yourself returning to)

The enum is closed (no other values accepted). If a doc genuinely
doesn't fit, default to `note`.

### `status` semantics

- **`active`** — file lives outside `archive/` directories; counts
  toward the live working set
- **`archived`** — file lives inside an `archive/` subdirectory;
  retained for history, not part of live work

Status MUST agree with location. The gate checks both directions.

### `project` semantics

- If file is in `<person>/random/` (or `random/archive/`), `project:
  null` is allowed.
- If file is in `<person>/proj-<shortname>/` (or its archive), `project:
  <shortname>` MUST match the folder shortname.
- Cross-references between WORKBENCH files in different projects
  should be by relative path; no upward references outside WORKBENCH.

### `owner` semantics

- `tim`, `van`, `shared` (extend as roster grows).
- `shared` means co-owned (e.g., handoff docs read by both).
- For agent-authored files, use the agent's owning-human's name OR
  `shared` if no specific human owns it.

### Date fields

- **`date_created`** — set once at file birth, IMMUTABLE. Manual edit
  forbidden.
- **`date_modified`** — auto-stamped by pre-commit hook on every
  staged commit. Manual edit forbidden.

If a file is created in WORKBENCH without dates, the pre-commit hook
populates both on first commit (one-time init). Subsequent commits
only update `date_modified`.

## Onboarding a new person

When a new human joins (e.g., when Van starts in bob):

1. Create `WORKBENCH/<person>/random/archive/` (full path; idempotent).
2. Optionally create `<person>/proj-<shortname>/archive/` for projects
   they'll work on.
3. Add the person to the roster section of `WORKBENCH/README.md`.

That's it. The standard requires NO ceremony for onboarding — just
the folder skeleton.

## What WORKBENCH is for

WORKBENCH is the build-surface. It exists to give a low-friction
space for:

- **Capture** — drop a note without ceremony, no PM entry needed
- **Exploration** — think out loud, try ideas that may not work
- **Multi-person async** — leave handoffs without scheduling
- **Drafting** — author content before it's ready for operating-surface
- **Reference** — keep external links/quotes for later

## What WORKBENCH is NOT for

- **Not a PM replacement** — coordination + state lives in the PM
  system. WORKBENCH < PM.
- **Not for canonical knowledge** — that goes in the operating-surface
  doc-types (per D-0066 matrix).
- **Not for client-deliverables** — WORKBENCH is venture-internal
  build-surface.
- **Not for permanent storage** — anything that should persist long-
  term needs to be PROMOTED to operating-surface (per
  [JOS-METHOD-WORKBENCH-LIFECYCLE]).

## Promotion to operating-surface

When a WORKBENCH file matures into canonical content (a decision, a
principle, a finalized doc), it undergoes a PROMOTION event. The
content gets synthesized into an operating-surface artifact with full
governance; the WORKBENCH source is archived.

See [JOS-METHOD-WORKBENCH-LIFECYCLE] for the procedure. Promotion is
deliberate, not automatic — `gold` and `discovery` files are the
common candidates.

## Connected Objects

- **[JOS-C47]** — Build-Surface vs Operating-Surface (foundation
  concept)
- **[JOS-D0066]** — Doc-Type × Thing-Type Matrix (WORKBENCH is a
  cell: REQ for venture/system/subsystem, OPT elsewhere)
- **[JOS-METHOD-WORKBENCH-LIFECYCLE]** — Lifecycle method (create →
  use → archive → promote)
- **[JOS-GATE-workbench-discipline]** — Gate that enforces this
  standard (warn-mode 90 days per SO-014, then blocking)
- **[JOS-SO-014]** — Coexistence pattern (90-day warn window for
  enforcement)
- **(JOSW2 Group C Item 19)** — Pre-commit hook auto-stamping
  `date_modified` on staged files
- **(JOSW2 Group C Item 20)** — `workbench_path` field on PM projects
  table for reverse lookup

## What Good Looks Like

A well-disciplined WORKBENCH a stranger lands in:

1. Has a `WORKBENCH/README.md` at the root explaining the folder
   shape and frontmatter spec
2. Has one folder per person on the team (`tim/`, `van/`)
3. Each person's folder contains exactly `random/` + per-project
   `proj-<shortname>/` subdirectories — no orphans
4. Every `.md` carries the 6-field frontmatter
5. `archive/` contents have `status: archived`; non-archive contents
   have `status: active`
6. `date_modified` matches the most recent commit (because the hook
   stamps it)
7. Files referencing PM projects have `project:` matching their
   folder name

## Drift-prevention

- **Don't manually edit `date_modified`.** The pre-commit hook owns
  it; manual edits get overwritten.
- **Don't create orphan folders.** Every subfolder under `<person>/`
  is `random/` OR `proj-<pm-shortname>/`.
- **Don't put venture-specific work in a meta-layer WORKBENCH.**
  (Incubator-host meta WORKBENCH is for portfolio admin only; per
  JOSW2 Group D.)
- **Don't ship WORKBENCH inside client-deliverable repos.** WORKBENCH
  is venture-internal.
- **Don't auto-promote files to operating-surface.** Promotion is a
  judgment call — use [JOS-METHOD-WORKBENCH-LIFECYCLE].
- **Don't treat existing files in random/ as canonical.** They're
  works-in-progress. If you find yourself relying on a random/ file
  as a source of truth, promote it.

## Coexistence with Foundry's WORKBENCH-STANDARD.md

Foundry-Agent-System has a working `WORKBENCH-STANDARD.md` that
predates this standard. Per JOSW2 Group B Item 13's
canonicalize-with-merge-extra-flavor rule:

1. Audit Foundry's WORKBENCH-STANDARD.md for any content not captured
   in this S32 (extra patterns, edge cases, examples).
2. Merge extra flavor UP into S32.
3. Replace Foundry's local copy with the projected canonical version.

Foundry then receives S32 via the standards projection (per JOSW2
Group B Item 7) instead of maintaining its own copy.

## Audit of existing WORKBENCH instances

Per JOSW2 Group C Item 21, audit existing WORKBENCH/ folders in:
- JOS itself (66 files; missing README/charter)
- Foundry-Agent-System (1071 files; reference implementation)
- venture-ecomlever (6 files; light version)
- venture-project-silk (stub)

Canonicalize per this standard. Backfill frontmatter where missing
(per JOSW2 Group C Item 22 — backfill `date_created` from git
history). Then activate the pre-commit hook (per JOSW2 Group C Item
19).
